reasoning:
The next Scottish Parliament election is on May 7, 2026. The latest polling (April 2026) shows the SNP heavily favored for first place (around 60-67 seats), removing the chance that Labour places 1st.
The battle for 2nd place is highly volatile and tightly contested between Scottish Labour, Reform UK, and sometimes the Scottish Greens. Labour's polling numbers have recently collapsed dramatically (now around 15-17%), tying or narrowly trailing Reform UK on the regional list vote. 
A highly-regarded YouGov MRP model published in April 2026 places Reform 2nd (20 seats) and Labour 3rd (17 seats). Survation polls in early 2026 had Labour and Reform tied on 18 seats. Another poll by The National / Find Out Now had Reform at 25 and Labour at 12. 
While Labour's vote might be slightly more regionally concentrated allowing for 1 or 2 constituency wins, their collapse in the list vote limits their seat ceiling significantly compared to the proportional momentum Reform carries. Taking the most recent tracking and seat translations into account, Reform looks favored to finish 2nd, while Labour finishes 3rd or 4th, with roughly a 25-30% chance for Labour to edge out Reform. My probability reflects the 2nd place tiebreaking between them. I'm providing an increasing uncertainty structure for further horizons, even though the election date is fixed for May 2026, to perfectly obey the format.
